Pantothenic acid is the amide between pantoic acid and β-alanine. Its name derives from the Greek ""pantothen"", meaning ""from everywhere"", and small quantities of pantothenic acid are found in nearly every food, with high amounts in fortified whole-grain cereals, egg yolks, liver and dried mushrooms.
